name: Kandy
summary: Kandy is generally safe for tourists and offers a rich cultural experience centered around the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic. The main safety concerns involve minor scams, tuk-tuk overcharging, and occasional instances of unwanted attention toward solo female travelers, though violent crime against tourists is rare.
quickVerdict: Kandy remains one of Sri Lanka's safer destinations with low rates of violent crime and a welcoming local population. While petty crime like pickpocketing can occur in crowded areas and tourist-targeted scams are relatively common, serious safety incidents involving visitors are uncommon. Solo female travelers generally feel secure, though some unwanted attention may occur. The city's main risks come from transportation-related issues, minor scams, and the need to respect local customs at religious sites.

neighborhoods.name: Kandy City Centre
neighborhoods.description: The heart of Kandy featuring the Temple of the Tooth, Kandy Lake, and main commercial areas. Well-patrolled with tourist police presence and good lighting. Generally safe day and night with regular foot traffic.

neighborhoods.name: Temple Area/Royal Palace Complex
neighborhoods.description: Highly secure area surrounding the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic with excellent security presence and constant tourist activity. Very safe for visitors of all backgrounds during all hours.

neighborhoods.name: Kandy Lake Surroundings
neighborhoods.description: Popular walking area with good lighting and regular patrols. Safe during day and early evening hours, though best to avoid very late night walks alone as the area becomes quieter.

neighborhoods.name: Market Areas
neighborhoods.description: Busy commercial areas near the central market can be crowded and have higher rates of pickpocketing. Generally safe but requires extra vigilance with belongings, especially during peak hours.

neighborhoods.name: Residential Suburbs
neighborhoods.description: Quiet residential areas outside the city center are generally peaceful and safe, though can be poorly lit at night. Safe during daytime but best to use transportation after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Outskirts/Rural Areas
neighborhoods.description: Areas on the outskirts of Kandy toward tea plantations and hills. While scenic, they have limited lighting and emergency services. Exercise caution when visiting alone, especially during monsoon season due to landslide risks.

scams.name: Tuk-Tuk Overcharging
scams.description: Drivers quote low fares initially but demand higher prices at destination, or take longer routes to increase costs. Some work with accomplices to intimidate tourists into paying excessive amounts.
scams.howToAvoid: Always negotiate and agree on price before starting journey. Use PickMe app for transparent pricing. Write agreed price on paper for confirmation. Walk away if driver won't negotiate reasonably.

scams.name: Fake Spice Garden Tours
scams.description: Tuk-tuk drivers bring tourists to overpriced spice gardens where fake doctors or professors pressure visitors to buy low-quality herbal remedies at inflated prices for commission.
scams.howToAvoid: Decline unsolicited spice garden visits. If interested, research reputable places independently. Don't feel obligated to buy anything. Tell your tuk-tuk driver you don't want spice garden stops.

scams.name: Gem Shop Scams
scams.description: Tourists are taken to government-approved gem shops selling overpriced or fake stones. Drivers and guides receive commissions while visitors pay inflated prices for low-quality gems.
scams.howToAvoid: Only buy gems from certified dealers with proper documentation. Avoid shops recommended by drivers or guides. Research gem quality beforehand. Don't believe promises about reselling gems for profit.

scams.name: Temple Donation Pressure
scams.description: Visitors may be pressured to make large mandatory donations at temples or religious sites, presented as required fees when they are actually optional contributions.
scams.howToAvoid: Understand that temple donations are voluntary. Politely decline excessive requests. Ask for official receipts for any payments. Research standard temple entry fees in advance.

soloFemale.overview: Kandy is considered one of the safer cities in Sri Lanka for solo female travelers, with most women reporting positive experiences. However, some unwanted attention and staring is common, particularly from men. While serious incidents are rare, it's important to dress modestly, especially when visiting religious sites, and take basic precautions after dark.
tips: Dress conservatively covering shoulders and knees, particularly around temples and in central areas
Use PickMe app or hotel-arranged taxis instead of random street tuk-tuks, especially at night
Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in central areas with good security
Be prepared for staring and occasional unwanted conversation - trust your instincts and remove yourself if uncomfortable
Avoid walking alone after 8 PM when the city becomes quieter, especially in poorly lit areas

nightSafety.overview: Kandy becomes significantly quieter after 8 PM with most areas shutting down early. While violent crime is uncommon, the reduced foot traffic and limited lighting in some areas increase vulnerability to petty crime and unwanted attention. The city center around the temple and lake areas maintain better security, but transportation becomes more important for safe movement after dark.
tips: Use tuk-tuks or hotel transportation for any travel after dark rather than walking
Stay in well-lit central areas near the temple and lake if out in the evening
Keep hotel contact information readily available and inform them of your plans
Avoid isolated areas and stick to main roads if you must walk at night
Be extra vigilant around the market areas where lighting may be poor

transport.metro: Kandy does not have a metro system. The city relies on buses, tuk-tuks, taxis, and the famous train connections to other parts of Sri Lanka.
transport.rideshare: PickMe app is available in Kandy and provides safer, more transparent pricing than street tuk-tuks. Uber has limited availability. Both are recommended over hailing random vehicles.
transport.taxis: Regular taxis are available but may not use meters. Hotel-arranged taxis are generally more reliable and safer. Three-wheelers (tuk-tuks) are the most common local transport but require price negotiation.
transport.tips: Always agree on tuk-tuk fares before starting your journey and consider writing the price down. Use the PickMe app when possible for transparent pricing. Avoid public buses for safety reasons as recommended by travel advisories. For train travel to other cities like Ella, book first or second class tickets in advance as the routes are very popular with tourists.

customs: Remove shoes and hats before entering Buddhist temples including the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic
Dress modestly with covered shoulders and knees when visiting religious sites - white clothing is preferred but not mandatory
Do not turn your back on Buddha statues when taking photos and never pose inappropriately with religious imagery
Greet with 'Ayubowan' (may you live long) and a slight bow rather than handshakes, especially with older locals
Show respect during religious ceremonies by remaining quiet and not taking flash photography

health.overview: Healthcare quality in Kandy varies between private and public facilities, with private hospitals offering better standards. The main health risks include dengue fever, chikungunya, and waterborne illnesses. Kandy has reported dengue cases and is in a high-risk area for vector-borne diseases.
health.water: Tap water is not recommended for drinking. Stick to bottled water or properly boiled water. Be cautious with ice in drinks and raw foods that may have been washed in local water.
health.vaccinations: Routine vaccinations should be up to date including MMR, DPT, and hepatitis A/B. Yellow fever vaccination required if arriving from endemic countries. Consider typhoid and Japanese encephalitis based on activities and length of stay.
health.altitude: Kandy sits at approximately 500 meters elevation so altitude sickness is not a concern. The tropical climate is hot and humid year-round with two monsoon seasons. Dengue and chikungunya cases have been reported in the Kandy area in 2025-2026.

emergency.usEmbassy: U.S. Embassy Colombo: +94-11-249-8500 (general), +94-11-202-8500 (24-hour emergency for American citizens). Located at 210 Galle Road, Colombo 03. For emergencies such as death, illness, arrest, or missing persons.

faq.a: Yes, Kandy is generally safe for tourists with low violent crime rates. The main concerns are minor scams, tuk-tuk overcharging, and petty theft in crowded areas. Most visitors have positive experiences when taking basic precautions.

Kandy becomes quieter after 8 PM and most areas shut down early. While serious crime is uncommon, reduced lighting and foot traffic increase vulnerability. Use tuk-tuks for transportation after dark and stick to well-lit central areas.

faq.q: Is Kandy safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Kandy is considered one of the safer Sri Lankan cities for solo female travelers. Expect some staring and occasional unwanted attention, but serious incidents are rare. Dress modestly, use reliable transportation, and trust your instincts.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Kandy?
faq.a: Avoid poorly lit market areas after dark, isolated outskirt areas especially during monsoon season, and be cautious in very crowded market spaces during peak hours where pickpocketing is more common.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Kandy?
faq.a: Trains to other cities are generally safe in first and second class, but avoid public buses due to safety concerns. Tuk-tuks are common but negotiate prices beforehand. Use PickMe app for more reliable and transparent pricing.
